doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_clear_underflow (mpfr/src/exceptions.c L152-L158). Three-line C body: `__gmpfr_flags &= MPFR_FLAGS_ALL ^ MPFR_FLAGS_UNDERFLOW` -- clear the UNDERFLOW bit (bit 0 = 1) from the global flag register. C returns void.

The TS port follows the same immutable-API contract used by the rest of the flag family (see mpfr_divby0_p, mpfr_nanflag_p): takes `mask: bigint` describing the pre-clear flag state, applies the clear-UNDERFLOW operation, returns the new flag state as `bigint`. Pure/stateless on the surface; exercises the same `flags &= ALL ^ BIT` arithmetic the C performs.

In-library wiring: clearFlags(MPFR_FLAGS_UNDERFLOW) from src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts.

Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply.
divergence_from_c: Three divergences: (1) return type C void -> TS bigint (new flag state); (2) param list C void -> TS `mask: bigint` (pre-clear state); (3) mechanism: clearFlags(MPFR_FLAGS_UNDERFLOW) or inline `mask & (63n ^ 1n) = mask & 62n`. Both correct. Reference port uses inline arithmetic for self-containment.
refs: mpfr/src/exceptions.c L152-L158 -- the C reference body.
src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts -- the TS flag register (already shipped).
